slides.title: Three reasons D14 beats the average

items.body: Sims Urban Oasis 1BR clears $3,150–$3,700/mo — high rent-to-price ratio vs CCR peers.
3BR units hitting $4,900–$5,500/mo on 800–900 sqft — premium psf rent with modest capital outlay.
Geylang-Paya Lebar corridor demand stays sticky; vacancy here runs tighter than D9/D10 luxury stock.

slides.source: URA caveats Q1 2026 · POV Guy analysis

slides.label: The yield trap to avoid
slides.punch: Wallich Residence 3BR at $11,300/mo sounds impressive — but at $4M+ purchase price, gross yield is under 3.4%. D14's Sims Urban Oasis 2BR at $3,900/mo on a $1.1M entry price is the smarter yield play.
slides.punch_bold: Chase yield efficiency, not headline rent.

slides.post: Highest single caveat in this dataset — 1,750 sqft at $10.29 psf/mo. D10 Nassim Rd bungalow: $62,000/mo.

slides.pre: Buying for yield in D14, D15 or D02?
slides.title: Let's map your

slides.title: Forward this to
slides.sub: If you know anyone building a rental portfolio in Singapore, send them this breakdown.

caption:
D14 is quietly one of Singapore's sharpest rental yield districts right now. Here's why.

• Sims Urban Oasis 2BR median rent hit S$3,900/mo in Q1 2026 — up ~39% vs 2 years ago
• Wallich Residence (D02) earns S$11,300–S$18,000/mo but gross yield sits under 3.4%
• D14 2BR on a ~S$1.1M entry price clears approx. 4.3% gross — one of the best in market

High headline rent isn't the same as high yield. The rent-to-price ratio is what moves the needle.
